summary: Iteration 1 — comprehensive backend + frontend testing of LAMAZI Sweets MVP. Backend: 26/26 pytest tests passed including Supabase reads, order creation (cash), admin CRUD (categories, coupons, loyalty, operating-hours, settings), validation rules. Tap LIVE charge create verified once and cleaned up (order cancelled). Frontend: all 7 customer pages + admin login render correctly; 6 menu items from Supabase render with images & bilingual names; Item Detail Modal opens with image/price/notes/qty; header badge increments after Add to Bag. Mobile hamburger correctly replaces desktop nav at 390px. CRITICAL BUG FOUND: cart contents are wiped on hard page navigation due to a useEffect ordering bug in CartContext.jsx — this breaks the entire bag → checkout flow.

minor.issue: Returns 404 for unknown code 'INVALID' (acceptable per spec). Test passes.

ui_bugs.issue: Cart is wiped on every hard page navigation. After clicking 'Add to bag' on /menu the header badge shows '1' correctly, but navigating to /bag shows 'Your bag is empty'. Root cause: two useEffects — effect-1 reads localStorage and calls setItems(saved), effect-2 writes JSON.stringify(items) to localStorage on every items change. On mount, effect-2 fires with the initial items=[] BEFORE effect-1's setItems has applied, overwriting the persisted cart with '[]'. Although effect-2 then re-fires after state updates, the visible /bag screen renders the empty state and the persisted cart can race-condition to empty on reload. Fix: gate the save-effect with a loaded flag (e.g. const [hydrated,setHydrated]=useState(false); set true in effect-1; in effect-2 if(!hydrated) return;).
ui_bugs.selector: data-testid for bag-empty-state not present; observed text 'Your bag is empty'

ui_bugs.issue: Because of the CartContext bug above, Bag does not list items, coupon input is not rendered (only shown when items exist), 'Proceed to Checkout' cannot be reached, and /checkout cannot be tested for delivery toggle / Leaflet map / payment toggle / Place Order in a real user flow via hard nav. (Note: these widgets are implemented in Checkout.jsx per code review; testing was blocked by the cart bug.)

issues: Card buttons say 'Add to Bag' (capital B) while modal CTA says 'Add to bag — 15.000 KWD' (lowercase b). Minor copy inconsistency.

action_items: After cart fix, re-test full bag → checkout → place order (cash) flow end-to-end via UI and confirm new Supabase order, then cleanup.
Minor: standardise 'Add to Bag' capitalisation between cards and modal CTA.
critical_code_review_comments: CartContext.jsx: two useEffects race on mount and can wipe persisted cart (see ui_bugs above). High-impact one-line fix.
server.py is 1268 lines — consider splitting into routers (menu, orders, admin, integrations) for maintainability (>700 line guideline).

Admin endpoints under /api/admin/* have NO backend auth middleware — protected only at the frontend via /api/admin/check email lookup. Spec confirms this is intentional for Phase 1 but should be hardened before public launch (anyone with the URL can read/write tenant data).
Guest coupon usage tracking via orders.notes ILIKE '*guest_id:X*coupon:Y*' is fragile — works but should move to a proper guest_id column or coupon_usage row with NULL customer_id + guest_id field.
Tap LIVE keys committed in /app/backend/.env — must be rotated before production handover (user is aware).

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — pages render, integration to backend works, but cart persistence broken on hard nav

test_credentials: No login required for tested endpoints. Admin login UI tested for render only (no credentials provided / admin user must be created manually in Supabase per spec).
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up: 1 cash order (cancelled), 1 Tap LIVE charge (order cancelled), 1 test category (deleted), 1 test coupon TESTCOUP10 (deleted), 1 test customer row (left in customers table — id is a random UUID with email starting test_<uuid>@example.com).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is /app/backend/tests/test_lamazi_api.py — runs in ~25s. Frontend critical-path retest needed after cart fix: (1) Menu /menu — click Add to Bag on first card, modal opens, click 'Add to bag — 15.000 KWD' inside modal, header badge shows 1; (2) hard navigate to /bag — items must remain visible, subtotal+delivery+total visible, coupon INVALID should show error toast; (3) Proceed to Checkout → /checkout — Leaflet map renders, delivery/takeaway toggle works, COD/Tap toggle works, Place Order with cash creates Supabase order. Selectors are NOT guaranteed via data-testid — most tests used text/role selectors. Suggest adding data-testid on: bag-item-row, bag-subtotal, bag-checkout-btn, checkout-payment-cod, checkout-payment-tap, checkout-place-order, item-modal, item-modal-add-button. Tap LIVE key still active — only do ONE charge create test maximum and cancel the order after.
rca_of_the_issue: CartContext.jsx mount sequence (verified via reproduction): step 1 — first render with items=[]. step 2 — React fires both useEffects in declaration order after commit. step 3 — effect-1 reads localStorage and calls setItems(savedItems); this only SCHEDULES a state update. step 4 — effect-2 runs immediately after with the CURRENT items=[] and writes JSON.stringify([]) into localStorage, OVERWRITING the persisted cart. step 5 — state update from step 3 lands → re-render → effect-2 re-fires with items=saved → writes correct value back. The empty write in step 4 is what produces the visible empty bag on hard navigation if the page reads cart before the second effect-2 re-run completes. Mitigation: gate the writer with a hydrated flag (see action_items).
